summary: Stellar Dark Star → Morning Dew and tight first‑set jams
blurb: The concert kicks off with a crisp, rock‑focused Truckin' that rolls into strong versions of Sugaree, Black Throated Wind and It Hurts Me Too. The second set delivers the tour‑defining Dark Star → Morning Dew followed by an energetic Good Lovin' and Casey Jones, while the encore closes with a heartfelt Sing Me Back Home.
review: May 18, 1972 – Munich’s Kongressaal Deutsches Museum captured one of Europe ’72’s most exhilarating nights. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is described as “stupendous, jazzy and emotive,” soaring especially during the piano‑guitar duel with Keith in the Dark Star that morphs into a searing Morning Dew, where his vocals shine. Phil Lesh locks in with gorgeous bass solos and even drops an España Cañí tuning on Tennessee Jed, while Bob Weir provides funky rhythm chops that lock with Garcia’s improvisations. The dual‑keyboard texture of Keith’s piano and Pigpen’s organ adds depth throughout, and Bill Kreutzmann punctuates the set with tasteful breaks after the climactic Morning Dew. The first set is tight and jazzy, building momentum through Truckin', Sugaree and Black Throated Wind, leading into a powerful second‑set peak that many fans cite as a highlight of the European ’72 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three available recordings are marked positive; reviewers repeatedly praise the Dark Star → Morning Dew, Good Lovin' jam and overall tightness, using language like “stupendous,” “manic,” “spectacular,” and “high‑energy.” Consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard mix that’s crisp, beautifully balanced and captures the Dark Star → Morning Dew segue with only minor tape hiss.

key_highlights: Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence is a tour‑defining improvisational peak.
Good Lovin' jam showcases Jerry’s emotive lead and tight band interplay.
First set tightness with standout Truckin', Sugaree, Black Throated Wind and It Hurts Me Too.
Dual‑keyboard texture from Keith Godchaux (piano) and Pigpen (organ) adds depth throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Lead guitar is repeatedly called stupendous, jazzy and emotive; manic soaring tone in Dark Star duel with Keith and a strong vocal on Morning Dew.
band_performance.Phil: Gorgeous bass solos locked in tight; notable España Cañí tuning on Tennessee Jed and “loaves” on Black Throated Wind.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm work shines, especially funky wailing alongside Garcia during Dark Star; solid throughout the set.
band_performance.Keith: Piano adds depth and drive, highlighted on Tennessee Jed, Good Lovin' and the piano‑guitar duel in Dark Star; blends seamlessly with Pigpen’s organ.
band_performance.Billy: Tasteful drum breaks after Morning Dew; overall powerful, trippy drumming that fuels the second‑set intensity.
